title: Livingstone Volunteers – McConnell International Foundation
text_excerpt: Livingstone Volunteers – McConnell International Foundation Skip to content Home Who We Are Menu Toggle Our Vision Our Trustees What We Do Menu Toggle Keep Girls in School Previous Projects Supporting Children Livingstone Volunteers News Fundraising Contact Us Donate Donate Main Menu Home Who We Are Menu Toggle Our Vision Our Trustees What We Do Menu Toggle Keep Girls in School Previous Projects Supporting Children Livingstone Volunteers News Fundraising Contact Us Donate Donate Personal or financial challenges should not exclude young people from volunteering Livingstone Volunteers The McConnell International Foundation believes that international volunteering can help Scottish youngsters widen their horizons, grow in confidence and understand the world a bit better. But not all young people have the opportunity to reap the benefits of volunteering abroad, particularly where the financial contribution required is substantial. In 2014, the Scottish Government financially supported a number of events to promote the legacy of David Livingstone, the 19th century missionary and explorer who played a pivotal in ending to the slave trade in East Africa, including Malawi. MIF successfully bid for one of the grants available and decided to establish an annual scheme that would help youngsters from Scotland who were facing disadvantages or challenges take part in volunteering opportunities in the countries most associated with Livingstone – Malawi, Zambia, Tanzania, and South Africa. The Foundation has established the Livingstone Volunteers scheme to help support the fundraising efforts of those young Scots who have the chance to volunteer in Africa but are struggling to meet the costs and run the scheme annually between 2014 and 2019. We were supported annually by the Scottish Government, the Sir Tom Hunter Foundation and a number of donors. Due to the Covid-19 pandemic and the restrictions placed upon international travel, the Trustees of the McConnell International Foundation took the difficult decision in early 2020 to suspend the Livingstone Volunteers. We will take the time available to review the scheme and modernise it for a post-Covid world. As part of this, we plan to initiate a forum with other partners on the ‘Future of Global Volunteering’. Should you wish to be involved in this forum, or for more information, please contact us on mcconnellintfoundation@gmail.com . Life changing opportunities 2014-2019 Livingstone Volunteers 2018 The fifth anniversary of the Livingstone Volunteers Scheme, and it did not disappoint. Thank you to all of our wonderful grant recipients, judging panel, and our supporters and donors. Want to learn what the Scheme is all about? Check out the 2018 report. Livingstone Volunteers 2017 Congratulations to all of our recipients, we were delighted to have you represent the scheme. To learn more about what our volunteers got up to, take a look at the 2017 report . Livingstone Volunteers 2016 The 2016 Livingstone Volunteers scheme was launched in October 2015. The 2016 scheme saw the highest number of full £500 grants being awarded in Livingstone Volunteers’ history and awarded these grants to young persons across Scotland, from Glasgow to Stirling to Fife to Aberdeen. The summary report on the Livingstone Volunteers 2016 is now able to view . Livingstone Volunteers 2015 The 2015 scheme focused on young people aged from 15 to 19 years who secured a volunteer placement on their school’s 2015 trip to sub-Saharan Eastern Africa, or with a similar organised group. Livingstone Volunteers 2015 saw a total of 20 applications, with 18 grants being awarded. An event was held in April 2015 to present each of the successful applicants with their grant. To read all about how are volunteers got on, visit the full report of the 2015 Scheme .
